mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the discount rate for equity valuations, increasing present values of future corporate earnings and generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS changes remains below 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows, or forward 20-day returns show no statistical significance against rate changes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained unemployment rate above 4.0 percent signals labor market slack that reduces wage inflation pressures and corporate profit margins, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 4.0 percent, invalidating the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength that drives corporate revenue growth, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns with an out-of-sample accuracy exceeding 52 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp contractions in the index of industrial production reflect immediate supply chain bottlenecks and reduced manufacturing output, triggering short-term risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Negative INDPRO month-over-month changes do not precede negative 5-day S&P 500 returns with a statistically significant directional accuracy above 50 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price index levels exceeding 250.0 indicate entrenched inflation that forces central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns remain positive in over 60 percent of observations when CPIAUCSL stays above 250.0, refuting the inflation penalty mechanism.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE yields a negative or zero Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows, indicating no predictive power for 60-day returns.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product levels reflect stronger aggregate economic activity that immediately boosts equity risk premiums and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDPC1 revisions show no statistically significant correlation with subsequent 5-day S&P 500 directional returns across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production falling below 105.0 signals a manufacturing downturn that reduces capital expenditure expectations and corporate profit forecasts, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in more than 58 percent of months where INDPRO remains below 105.0, invalidating the supply-side drag hypothesis.
